716	| `EndInput`     | _(DateField)_ | End date field                                                      |
717	| `Trigger`      | `<button>`    | `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`          |
718	| `ClearTrigger` | `<button>`    | `aria-label="Clear date range"`                                     |
719	| `Positioner`   | `<div>`       | Floating positioner (via positioning engine)                        |
720	| `Content`      | `<div>`       | `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="false"`, `aria-labelledby`            |
721	| `Description`  | `<div>`       | Help text, wired via `aria-describedby`                             |
722	| `ErrorMessage` | `<div>`       | `role="alert"`, validation error                                    |
723	| `HiddenInput`  | `<input>`     | `type="hidden"`, ISO 8601 range value                               |
724	
725	## 3. Accessibility
726	
727	### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties
728	
729	| Element        | Role/Attribute      | Details                                                                       |
730	| -------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
731	| `Control`      | `role="group"`      | `aria-labelledby` points to `Label`                                           |
732	| `Trigger`      | `<button>`          | `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls` points to Content  |
733	| `Content`      | `role="dialog"`     | `aria-modal="false"`, `aria-labelledby` points to Label                       |
734	| `ErrorMessage` | `role="alert"`      | Announced immediately on render                                               |
735	| `Root`         | --                  | `data-ars-invalid` when validation fails                                      |
736	| `StartInput`   | spinbutton segments | `aria-label` from `messages.start_label`; delegates to DateField ARIA pattern |
737	| `EndInput`     | spinbutton segments | `aria-label` from `messages.end_label`; delegates to DateField ARIA pattern   |
738	
739	### 3.2 Keyboard Interaction
740	
741	| Key         | Context         | Action                                        |
742	| ----------- | --------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
743	| `ArrowDown` | Trigger / Input | Open the popover                              |
744	| `Escape`    | Popover open    | Close the popover, restore focus to trigger   |
745	| `Tab`       | Within calendar | Close the popover                             |
746	| `Enter`     | Calendar cell   | Complete range selection (if second endpoint) |
747	| `Space`     | Calendar cell   | Complete range selection (if second endpoint) |
748	
749	### 3.3 Focus Flow
750	
751	- When the popover opens, focus moves into the Calendar grid.
752	- When a range is completed, the popover closes and focus returns to the trigger.
753	- Start and End inputs are linked via `aria-labelledby` to the shared label.
754	- Tab navigation flows: StartInput -> Separator (skipped) -> EndInput -> Trigger -> ClearTrigger.
755	- `aria-describedby` wires to Description and ErrorMessage parts when present.
756	
757	## 4. Internationalization
758	
759	### 4.1 Messages
760	
761	```rust
762	/// Messages for the DateRangePicker component.
763	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
764	pub struct Messages {
765	    /// Trigger button label (default: "Open date range picker").
766	    pub trigger_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
767	    /// Label for the start date field.
768	    pub start_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
769	    /// Label for the end date field.
770	    pub end_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
771	    /// Describes the selected range (e.g. "March 1 to March 15").
772	    pub range_description: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str, &str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
773	    /// Accessible label for the clear trigger button (default: "Clear date range").
774	    pub clear_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
775	}
776	
777	impl Default for Messages {
778	    fn default() -> Self {
779	        Self {
780	            trigger_label: MessageFn::static_str("Open date range picker"),
781	            start_label: MessageFn::static_str("Start date"),
782	            end_label: MessageFn::static_str("End date"),
783	            range_description: MessageFn::new(|start, end, _locale| format!("{} to {}", start, end)),
784	            clear_label: MessageFn::static_str("Clear date range"),
785	        }
786	    }
787	}
788	
789	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
790	```
791	
792	## 5. Form Integration
793	
794	- Hidden `<input>` submits the range as two ISO 8601 dates separated by `/` (e.g., `2024-01-10/2024-01-20`).
795	- `name` attribute set from Props.
796	- Reset restores `default_value`.
797	- Validation states reflected via `data-ars-invalid` on Root.
798	- `aria-describedby` wires to Description and ErrorMessage parts.
799	- `aria-required` set when `required` is true.
800	- Disabled/readonly propagation from form context per `07-forms.md` S15.
801	
802	## 6. Library Parity
803	
804	> Compared against: Ark UI (`DatePicker` with `selectionMode: 'range'`), React Aria (`DateRangePicker`).
805	
806	### 6.1 Props
807	
808	| Feature               | ars-ui            | Ark UI                | React Aria                  | Notes                                               |
809	| --------------------- | ----------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
810	| Controlled range      | `value`           | `value` (DateValue[]) | `value` (RangeValue)        | Equivalent                                          |
811	| Default range         | `default_value`   | `defaultValue`        | `defaultValue`              | Equivalent                                          |
812	| Min/max               | `min`, `max`      | `min`, `max`          | `minValue`, `maxValue`      | Equivalent                                          |
813	| Disabled              | `disabled`        | `disabled`            | `isDisabled`                | Equivalent                                          |
814	| Read-only             | `readonly`        | `readOnly`            | `isReadOnly`                | Equivalent                                          |
815	| Required              | `required`        | `required`            | `isRequired`                | Equivalent                                          |
816	| Close on select       | `close_on_select` | `closeOnSelect`       | `shouldCloseOnSelect`       | Equivalent                                          |
817	| Locale                | `locale`          | `locale`              | -- (context)                | Equivalent                                          |
818	| Format                | `format`          | `format`              | --                          | Equivalent                                          |
819	| Name                  | `name`            | `name`                | `startName`, `endName`      | React Aria has separate form names                  |
820	| Positioning           | `positioning`     | `positioning`         | --                          | Equivalent                                          |
821	| Allows non-contiguous | --                | --                    | `allowsNonContiguousRanges` | React Aria feature                                  |
822	| Max visible months    | --                | `numOfMonths`         | `maxVisibleMonths`          | ars-ui uses `visible_months: 2` default on Calendar |
823	| Start/end form names  | --                | --                    | `startName`, `endName`      | React Aria has separate form names for start/end    |
824	
825	**Gaps:**
826	
827	- `allowsNonContiguousRanges`: React Aria allows selecting ranges that span unavailable dates. Currently ars-ui's RangeCalendar blocks selection of unavailable dates. This is a niche feature; not adopting.
828	
829	### 6.2 Anatomy
830	
831	| Part          | ars-ui         | Ark UI            | React Aria                 | Notes                               |
832	| ------------- | -------------- | ----------------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
833	| Root          | `Root`         | `Root`            | `DateRangePicker`          | Equivalent                          |
834	| Label         | `Label`        | `Label`           | `Label`                    | Equivalent                          |
835	| Control       | `Control`      | `Control`         | `Group`                    | Equivalent                          |
836	| Start input   | `StartInput`   | `Input` (index=0) | `DateInput` (slot="start") | Equivalent                          |
837	| Separator     | `Separator`    | --                | --                         | ars-ui adds a visual separator      |
838	| End input     | `EndInput`     | `Input` (index=1) | `DateInput` (slot="end")   | Equivalent                          |
839	| Trigger       | `Trigger`      | `Trigger`         | `Button`                   | Equivalent                          |
840	| Clear trigger | `ClearTrigger` | `ClearTrigger`    | --                         | ars-ui and Ark UI have clear button |
841	| Positioner    | `Positioner`   | `Positioner`      | --                         | Equivalent                          |
842	| Content       | `Content`      | `Content`         | `Popover` + `Dialog`       | Equivalent                          |
843	| Description   | `Description`  | --                | `Text` (slot)              | Equivalent                          |
844	| Error message | `ErrorMessage` | --                | `FieldError`               | Equivalent                          |
845	| Hidden input  | `HiddenInput`  | --                | --                         | ars-ui form integration             |
846	
847	**Gaps:** None worth adopting.
848	
849	### 6.3 Events
850	
851	| Callback     | ars-ui                 | Ark UI          | React Aria      | Notes      |
852	| ------------ | ---------------------- | --------------- | --------------- | ---------- |
853	| Range change | `SelectRangeComplete`  | `onValueChange` | `onChange`      | Equivalent |
854	| Open change  | `Open` / `Close`       | `onOpenChange`  | `onOpenChange`  | Equivalent |
855	| Focus change | `FocusIn` / `FocusOut` | `onFocusChange` | `onFocusChange` | Equivalent |
856	
857	**Gaps:** None.
858	
859	### 6.4 Features
860	
861	| Feature               | ars-ui                      | Ark UI            | React Aria            |
862	| --------------------- | --------------------------- | ----------------- | --------------------- |
863	| Two-field input       | Yes (StartInput + EndInput) | Yes (Input index) | Yes (start/end slots) |
864	| Calendar popover      | Yes                         | Yes               | Yes                   |
865	| Range normalization   | Yes                         | Yes               | Yes                   |
866	| Close on select       | Yes                         | Yes               | Yes                   |
867	| Clear button          | Yes                         | Yes               | No                    |
868	| Multi-month calendar  | Yes (default 2)             | Yes               | Yes                   |
869	| Non-contiguous ranges | No                          | No                | Yes                   |
870	| Separate form names   | No                          | No                | Yes                   |
871	| Hidden form input     | Yes                         | No                | No                    |
872	
873	**Gaps:** None.
874	
875	### 6.5 Summary
876	
877	- **Overall:** Full parity.
878	- **Divergences:** React Aria supports `allowsNonContiguousRanges` which is a niche feature not adopted. ars-ui submits ranges via a single hidden input while React Aria uses separate form names.
879	- **Recommended additions:** None.
880	
881	## Appendix: Testing
882	
883	```rust

885	mod tests {
886	    use super::*;
887	
888	    #[test]
889	    fn initial_state_is_closed() {
890	        let (state, ctx) = Machine::init(&Props::default(), &Env::default(), &Default::default());
891	        assert_eq!(state, State::Closed);

893	        assert!(ctx.value.get().is_none());
894	    }
895

901	        assert_eq!(plan.target, Some(State::Open));
902	
903	        let (state, ctx) = (State::Open, /* apply plan */);
904	        let plan = Machine::transition(&state, &Event::Close, &ctx, &props).unwrap();
905	        assert_eq!(plan.target, Some(State::Closed));
906	    }
907	
908	    #[test]
909	    fn select_range_complete_closes_and_sets_value() {
910	        let props = Props::default();

912	        let range = DateRange::new(
913	            CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025, nzu8(3), nzu8(1)),
914	            CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025, nzu8(3), nzu8(15)),
915	        );
916	        let plan = Machine::transition(

924

936	        assert!(plan.target.is_none()); // context-only
937	    }
938	
939	    #[test]

950	    }

content.text:
I now have the complete picture. `range_calendar::Props` impls `Default` (so I can spread it), and `date_range_field` formats dates via an `intl_backend` for `range_description`. 

Here's the situation: **the DateRangePicker spec has substantial drift from the real dependency APIs**, because it was written before `date_field` and `range_calendar` were finalized. The spec models the inputs as text fields with a `format: DateFormat` string and parses raw strings, and composes `calendar { is_range: true }`. In reality:

- `date_field` is a **segmented** field (emits `Option<CalendarDate>`, has no `format` string — uses `calendar`/`granularity`/`segment_order`); `DateFormat` doesn't exist.
- Range selection lives in the dedicated **`range_calendar`** component (dependency #287); `calendar::Props` has no `is_range`/`range_start`/`range_end`.
- `CalendarDate::to_iso_string()` doesn't exist (it's `to_iso8601()`); `DateRange::normalized()` returns `Option`, so the spec's `set(Some(normalized(...)))` is a type error.
- `range_calendar` requires a `today` date; the spec has no `today` prop. `PositioningOptions` exists but DatePicker (the analog) omits it (positioning is an adapter concern per the issue's element-handling note).

The proven sibling `date_range_field` already establishes the correct value-based pattern (`StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`, `recompute_range` via `DateRange::normalized`). Let me confirm two decisions before writing the plan.

questions.question: The spec's §1 models the two inputs as text fields (StartInputChange { value: String }, format: DateFormat, in-core string parsing) but the real date_field is segmented and emits Option<CalendarDate>, mirroring the existing date_range_field. How should DateRangePicker model the two inputs, and may I amend spec §1.2-1.9/§2 to match the real dependency APIs in this PR?

options.label: Value-based, amend spec
options.description: Compose real date_field via start_field_props()/end_field_props(); events StartValueChange/EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>); recompute range via DateRange::normalized (mirrors date_range_field). Compose range_calendar (not calendar{is_range}). Rewrite the drifted spec sections to match. Matches anatomy 'StartInput (DateField)'.

options.label: Text-based in-core parsing
options.description: Keep the spec's StartInputChange { value: String } + add format_date/parse_date string parsing in the core (like DatePicker's single input). The StartInput/EndInput parts would NOT be real DateFields, diverging from the spec anatomy and date_range_field.

questions.question: The issue's 'Tests to add first' lists preset support (Last 7 days, Last 30 days, custom presets), but the spec defines no preset API and the Library Parity section (§6) lists no presets (neither Ark UI DatePicker nor React Aria DateRangePicker has built-in presets). Inventing one would add un-specced public API. How should presets be handled?

options.label: Omit presets
options.description: Do not implement a preset API. It is not in spec §1/§2 (the acceptance-criteria contract) nor §6 parity. Presets are user-land composition: consumers set value to a computed range. Note this in the PR body as a deliberate scope decision vs the epic's aspirational test list.

options.label: Add a preset API
options.description: Design and implement a presets feature (e.g. a Vec of named ranges) plus spec it. This adds public surface beyond the current spec and parity targets, and enlarges the task beyond its 5 points.

input.content:
# Task #290 — Implement DateRangePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #290 (epic #224, 5 pts) asks for the framework-agnostic core of **DateRangePicker**:
a popover composing **two DateField inputs** + a **RangeCalendar** grid, with bidirectional
range coordination, validation, form integration, and ARIA/data attrs. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) and all live DOM concerns (focus, positioning, scroll, return-focus) are out of
scope. Dependencies #284 (DateRangeField), #287 (RangeCalendar), #289 (DatePicker) are all merged.

### Spec drift (resolved in this PR per the spec-synchronization rule)

The spec (`spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md`) predates the finalized dependency
APIs and will not compile as written. The user approved **amending the spec to match the real
APIs** (value-based fields) and **adding a preset API**. Confirmed-against-code corrections:

| Spec as written | Reality | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| `format: DateFormat`, `format_date`/`parse_date`, `start_input_text`/`parsed_start` | `DateFormat` does not exist; `date_field` is segmented and emits `Option<CalendarDate>` (fields: `value`, `min_value`, `max_value`, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`; no `format`) | Drop `format`/text model. Store `start_date`/`end_date: Option<CalendarDate>`. Events `StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` / `EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` (mirror `date_range_field`) |
| `calendar::Props { is_range: true, range_start, range_end }` | `calendar::Props` has no `is_range`; range lives in the dedicated **`range_calendar`** component (#287) | `range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` |
| `CalendarDate::to_iso_string()` | Method is `to_iso8601()`; `DateRange::to_iso8601()` yields `"start/end"` | Use `range.to_iso8601()` for combined hidden input; `range.start.to_iso8601()` for split |
| `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(a,b)))` | `normalized()` returns `Option<DateRange>` (type error) | `match DateRange::normalized(a,b) { Some(r)=>set(Some(r)), None=>set(None) }` (mirror `date_range_field::recompute_range`) |
| No `today` prop | `range_calendar::Props.today` is required | Add `today: CalendarDate` prop, forward to `range_calendar_props()` (mirror `date_picker`) |
| `positioning: PositioningOptions` | Type exists but DatePicker omits it; positioning is an adapter concern per the issue's element-handling note | Drop from agnostic Props |
| Machine omits `type Effect`, no `SyncProps`, no `Clear` event | `Machine::Effect` is non-defaulted; controlled `value` + `ClearTrigger` part need wiring | Add `type Effect = NoEffect`, `Event::SyncProps(Box<Props>)` + `on_props_changed`, `Event::Clear` |

## Design

DateRangePicker owns the canonical range and popover state; it does **not** run child machines.
Like DatePicker, it exposes `*_props()` builders the adapter feeds to child machines and accepts
bridged events. The two-field value logic mirrors the proven `date_range_field`; the popover
open/close mirrors `date_picker`.

**State** (unchanged): `Closed`, `Open`.

**Events** (amended): `Open`, `Close`, `Toggle`, `SelectRangeComplete { range: DateRange }`,
`StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`, `EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`,
`SelectPreset { index: usize }`, `Clear`, `FocusIn`, `FocusOut`, `KeyDown { key: KeyboardKey }`,
`SyncProps(Box<Props>)`.

**Context** (amended): `value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>`, `open: Bindable<bool>`,
`start_date`/`end_date: Option<CalendarDate>`, `active_field: ActiveField`,
`min`/`max: Option<CalendarDate>`, `today: CalendarDate`, `presets: Vec<Preset>`,
`visible_months: usize`, `is_rtl: bool`, `locale: Locale`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`
(for `range_description` formatting), `messages: Messages`, `disabled`/`readonly`/`required`,
`force_leading_zeros: bool`, `has_description`/`has_error_message: bool`,
`name`/`start_name`/`end_name: Option<String>`, `ids: ComponentIds`.

**Props** (amended): `id`, `value: Option<Option<DateRange>>`, `default_value`, `min`, `max`,
`today` (default `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025,1,1)` like DatePicker), `presets: Vec<Preset>`,
`visible_months: usize` (default `2`), `is_rtl`, `disabled`, `readonly`, `required`,
`force_leading_zeros`, `has_description`, `has_error_message`, `name`, `start_name`, `end_name`,
`close_on_select: bool` (default `true`). Derives `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default, HasId`.

**New public `Preset` type** (deterministic, no closures — consumer supplies a pre-localized
label + a concrete range computed relative to the same injected `today`):
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct Preset {
    pub label: String,
    pub range: DateRange,
}
```

**Anatomy `Part`** (amended — adds data-carrying `PresetTrigger`, mirroring
`range_calendar::Part::Row { week_index }`):
`Root, Label, Control, StartInput, Separator, EndInput, Trigger, ClearTrigger,
PresetTrigger { index: usize }, Positioner, Content, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput`.

**Transition logic:**
- `disabled` guard returns `None` for all events except `SyncProps` (mirror `date_range_field`).
- `Open`/`Toggle` (Closed) → Open; `Close`/`Toggle` (Open) → Closed.
- `SelectRangeComplete { range }`: set `start_date`/`end_date`/`value`; close iff `props.close_on_select`.
- `SelectPreset { index }`: look up `ctx.presets[index]`, apply its range like `SelectRangeComplete`.
- `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`: update the field, set `active_field`,
  recompute range via `DateRange::normalized` (None when incomplete/incomparable). Blocked when `readonly`.
- `Clear`: clear `value`/`start_date`/`end_date`.
- `KeyDown` Escape (Open) → Close; ArrowDown (Closed) → Open.
- `FocusOut` (Open) → Close. `FocusIn` → `None` (kept for adapter-symmetry/spec parity).
- `SyncProps`: re-apply controlled `value` via `Bindable::sync_controlled`; refresh cached
  `min`/`max`/`today`/`presets`/flags (mirror `date_range_field::sync_props`). `open` is always uncontrolled.

**Api** (amended, no live-DOM ops): `root_attrs` (incl. `data-ars-invalid` when range out of
bounds — reuse `date_range_field`'s `date_out_of_bounds` check), `label_attrs` (`for={id}-start`),
`control_attrs` (`role=group`), `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`),
`start_field_props()`/`end_field_props() -> date_field::Props` (real fields: `value`, `min_value`,
`max_value` bounded by the sibling field, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`, `invalid`),
`trigger_attrs`, `clear_trigger_attrs` (disabled when no value/disabled),
`preset_trigger_attrs(index)` + `presets()` + `preset_label(index)`,
`positioner_attrs`, `content_attrs` (`role=dialog`, `aria-modal=false`, `aria-labelledby`),
`description_attrs`, `error_message_attrs` (`role=alert`),
`hidden_input_attrs` (`range.to_iso8601()`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs`,
`range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` (`..Default::default()` + value/min/max/today/
visible_months/is_rtl), `range_description() -> Option<String>` (via shared `format_date_label`),
imperative `open/close/toggle/clear/is_open/selected_range`, and `on_*` dispatch helpers.
`ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatches every `Part`.

**Messages** (per spec §4.1, unchanged): `trigger_label`, `start_label`, `end_label`,
`range_description: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str,&str,&Locale)->String + Send + Sync>`, `clear_label`.
Add `preset` labels come from `Preset.label`, not Messages.

## Files

Create:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/mod.rs` — machine, `State`, `Event`,
  `ActiveField`, `Context`, `Props`, `Preset`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. Directory-backed module.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/snapshots/*.snap` — committed insta fixtures.

Modify:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs` — add `pub mod date_range_picker;`; promote the
  duplicated private `format_date_label(date, &dyn IntlBackend, &Locale)` to a `pub(crate)` shared
  helper here and have `date_range_field`, `range_calendar`, and the new module call it (dedup;
  identical signature already exists in both siblings).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` and `.../range_calendar/mod.rs` —
  use the shared `format_date_label` (remove their local copies).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs` — add
  `date_range_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 14 parts (use a default index for
  `PresetTrigger { index: 0 }`, mirroring how `Row`/`Cell` are listed).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs` — add a `date_range_picker`
  arbitrary-event strategy + invariant block (mirror the existing `range_calendar`/`date_range_field` entries).
- `spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md` — rewrite §1.2 Events, §1.3 Context, §1.4 Props,
  §1.8 Machine, §1.9 Connect/API, §2 Anatomy (add `PresetTrigger`), and update §1 intro/§6 parity to
  match the value-based composition + the new `Preset` API; fix all the drift rows in the table above.

No `lib.rs`, `manifest.toml`, or catalog changes (component already specced/registered;
accessed via `ars_components::date_time::date_range_picker`).

## Workflow

1. Move issue #290 to **In Progress** on the GitHub Project board.
2. **TDD:** write the named tests first (unit + ARIA + snapshot) referencing the amended API, watch
   them fail, then implement `mod.rs` to green. Amend the spec alongside the implementation so §1/§2
   stay the contract.
3. Reuse, do not reinvent: `DateRange`/`CalendarDate`/`IntlBackend` from `ars_i18n`;
   `date_field::Props` (segmented fields); `range_calendar::Props`; `Bindable`,
   `ComponentIds`, `MessageFn`, `NoEffect`, `TransitionPlan`, `ConnectApi` from `ars_core`;
   `date_range_field`'s `recompute_range`/`date_out_of_bounds`/`sync_props` patterns;
   the shared `format_date_label`.
4. Zero warnings, `missing_docs` satisfied on every public item.
5. **MANDATORY** `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases; every finding fixed in this PR).
6. Present for review before any commit.
7. After approval: `cargo xci-fast`; fix failures; commit + push PR to `main` with `Closes #290`.
8. Add `snapshot-reviewed` label (this PR creates `.snap` fixtures).
9. **MANDATORY** `waiting-for-codex-review` skill after first push and after each subsequent push.
10. Merge only after green CI + Codex 👍; then close #290.

## Tests to add (named, first)

Unit (transition): `initial_state_is_closed`; `open_and_close`; `toggle`;
`select_range_complete_sets_value_and_closes`; `select_range_complete_stays_open_when_not_close_on_select`;
`start_value_change_recomputes_range`; `end_value_change_recomputes_range`;
`field_edit_syncs_into_range` (value sync: editing a field updates the canonical range/calendar highlight);
`range_normalizes_when_start_after_end`; `out_of_bounds_marks_invalid`;
`select_preset_applies_range_and_closes`; `clear_resets_value`; `escape_closes`; `arrow_down_opens`;
`focusout_closes`; `disabled_ignores_events`; `readonly_blocks_selection_allows_open`;
`controlled_value_syncs_via_set_props`.

ARIA/Api: `trigger_haspopup_expanded_controls`; `clear_trigger_disabled_when_empty`;
`content_dialog_attrs`; `error_message_alert_role`; `hidden_input_iso_range` (two hidden inputs:
combined `start/end` + split `start_name`/`end_name`); `start_field_props_bounds_and_label`;
`end_field_props_bounds_and_label`; `range_calendar_props_forwards_today_and_visible_months`;
`preset_trigger_attrs`; `range_description_when_complete`.

Snapshot (`assert_snapshot!`, `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`): every anatomy part and each
output-affecting branch — root (closed/open/disabled/readonly/required/invalid), label, control,
separator, trigger (closed/open/disabled), clear-trigger (empty/with-value), preset-trigger,
positioner, content, description, error-message, hidden-input (empty/with-range), split start/end
hidden inputs, and `start_field_props`/`end_field_props` rendered as `AttrMap`.

Proptest: DateRangePicker added to `proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs` (no panics; `value`
stays normalized; `open`↔state consistency).

Spec-conformance: `date_range_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` (14 parts, kebab names).

## Verification

- `cargo test -p ars-components date_range_picker` — all named unit/ARIA/snapshot tests pass, zero warnings.
- `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` — snapshots committed and referenced.
- `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance` and `--test proptest_state_machines`.
- `cargo xtask spec validate` — spec frontmatter still consistent after the amendment.
- `cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-components --text -- date_range_picker` — confirm new code meets the
  crate coverage threshold (no uncovered branches beyond no-op test closures).
- Final gate before push: `cargo xci-fast`.

`force_leading_zeros`, `has_description`, `has_error_message`, `name`, `start_name`, `end_name`,
`close_on_select: bool` (default `true`). Derives `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default, HasId`.

**New public `Preset` type** (deterministic, no closures — consumer supplies a pre-localized
label + a concrete range computed relative to the same injected `today`):
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct Preset {
    pub label: String,
    pub range: DateRange,
}
```

**Anatomy `Part`** (amended — adds data-carrying `PresetTrigger`, mirroring
`range_calendar::Part::Row { week_index }`):
`Root, Label, Control, StartInput, Separator, EndInput, Trigger, ClearTrigger,
PresetTrigger { index: usize }, Positioner, Content, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput`.

**Transition logic:**
- `disabled` guard returns `None` for all events except `SyncProps` (mirror `date_range_field`).
- `Open`/`Toggle` (Closed) → Open; `Close`/`Toggle` (Open) → Closed.
- `SelectRangeComplete { range }`: set `start_date`/`end_date`/`value`; close iff `props.close_on_select`.
- `SelectPreset { index }`: look up `ctx.presets[index]`, apply its range like `SelectRangeComplete`.
- `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`: update the field, set `active_field`,
  recompute range via `DateRange::normalized` (None when incomplete/incomparable). Blocked when `readonly`.
- `Clear`: clear `value`/`start_date`/`end_date`.
- `KeyDown` Escape (Open) → Close; ArrowDown (Closed) → Open.
- `FocusOut` (Open) → Close. `FocusIn` → `None` (kept for adapter-symmetry/spec parity).
- `SyncProps`: re-apply controlled `value` via `Bindable::sync_controlled`; refresh cached
  `min`/`max`/`today`/`presets`/flags (mirror `date_range_field::sync_props`). `open` is always uncontrolled.

**Api** (amended, no live-DOM ops): `root_attrs` (incl. `data-ars-invalid` when range out of
bounds — reuse `date_range_field`'s `date_out_of_bounds` check), `label_attrs` (`for={id}-start`),
`control_attrs` (`role=group`), `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`),
`start_field_props()`/`end_field_props() -> date_field::Props` (real fields: `value`, `min_value`,
`max_value` bounded by the sibling field, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`, `invalid`),
`trigger_attrs`, `clear_trigger_attrs` (disabled when no value/disabled),
`preset_trigger_attrs(index)` + `presets()` + `preset_label(index)`,
`positioner_attrs`, `content_attrs` (`role=dialog`, `aria-modal=false`, `aria-labelledby`),
`description_attrs`, `error_message_attrs` (`role=alert`),
`hidden_input_attrs` (`range.to_iso8601()`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs`,
`range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` (`..Default::default()` + value/min/max/today/
visible_months/is_rtl), `range_description() -> Option<String>` (via shared `format_date_label`),
imperative `open/close/toggle/clear/is_open/selected_range`, and `on_*` dispatch helpers.
`ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatches every `Part`.

**Messages** (per spec §4.1, unchanged): `trigger_label`, `start_label`, `end_label`,
`range_description: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str,&str,&Locale)->String + Send + Sync>`, `clear_label`.
Add `preset` labels come from `Preset.label`, not Messages.

## Files

Create:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/mod.rs` — machine, `State`, `Event`,
  `ActiveField`, `Context`, `Props`, `Preset`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. Directory-backed module.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/snapshots/*.snap` — committed insta fixtures.

Modify:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs` — add `pub mod date_range_picker;`; promote the
  duplicated private `format_date_label(date, &dyn IntlBackend, &Locale)` to a `pub(crate)` shared
  helper here and have `date_range_field`, `range_calendar`, and the new module call it (dedup;
  identical signature already exists in both siblings).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` and `.../range_calendar/mod.rs` —
  use the shared `format_date_label` (remove their local copies).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs` — add
  `date_range_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 14 parts (use a default index for
  `PresetTrigger { index: 0 }`, mirroring how `Row`/`Cell` are listed).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs` — add a `date_range_picker`
  arbitrary-event strategy + invariant block (mirror the existing `range_calendar`/`date_range_field` entries).
- `spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md` — rewrite §1.2 Events, §1.3 Context, §1.4 Props,
  §1.8 Machine, §1.9 Connect/API, §2 Anatomy (add `PresetTrigger`), and update §1 intro/§6 parity to
  match the value-based composition + the new `Preset` API; fix all the drift rows in the table above.

No `lib.rs`, `manifest.toml`, or catalog changes (component already specced/registered;
accessed via `ars_components::date_time::date_range_picker`).

## Workflow

1. Move issue #290 to **In Progress** on the GitHub Project board.
2. **TDD:** write the named tests first (unit + ARIA + snapshot) referencing the amended API, watch
   them fail, then implement `mod.rs` to green. Amend the spec alongside the implementation so §1/§2
   stay the contract.
3. Reuse, do not reinvent: `DateRange`/`CalendarDate`/`IntlBackend` from `ars_i18n`;
   `date_field::Props` (segmented fields); `range_calendar::Props`; `Bindable`,
   `ComponentIds`, `MessageFn`, `NoEffect`, `TransitionPlan`, `ConnectApi` from `ars_core`;
   `date_range_field`'s `recompute_range`/`date_out_of_bounds`/`sync_props` patterns;
   the shared `format_date_label`.
4. Zero warnings, `missing_docs` satisfied on every public item.
5. **MANDATORY** `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases; every finding fixed in this PR).
6. Present for review before any commit.
7. After approval: `cargo xci-fast`; fix failures; commit + push PR to `main` with `Closes #290`.

9. **MANDATORY** `waiting-for-codex-review` skill after first push and after each subsequent push.
10. Merge only after green CI + Codex 👍; then close #290.

Unit (transition): `initial_state_is_closed`; `open_and_close`; `toggle`;
`select_range_complete_sets_value_and_closes`; `select_range_complete_stays_open_when_not_close_on_select`;
`start_value_change_recomputes_range`; `end_value_change_recomputes_range`;
`field_edit_syncs_into_range` (value sync: editing a field updates the canonical range/calendar highlight);

`select_preset_applies_range_and_closes`; `clear_resets_value`; `escape_closes`; `arrow_down_opens`;
`focusout_closes`; `disabled_ignores_events`; `readonly_blocks_selection_allows_open`;
`controlled_value_syncs_via_set_props`.

ARIA/Api: `trigger_haspopup_expanded_controls`; `clear_trigger_disabled_when_empty`;
`content_dialog_attrs`; `error_message_alert_role`; `hidden_input_iso_range` (two hidden inputs:
combined `start/end` + split `start_name`/`end_name`); `start_field_props_bounds_and_label`;
`end_field_props_bounds_and_label`; `range_calendar_props_forwards_today_and_visible_months`;
`preset_trigger_attrs`; `range_description_when_complete`.

# Task #290 — Implement DateRangePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #290 (epic #224, 5 pts) asks for the framework-agnostic core of **DateRangePicker**:
a popover composing **two DateField inputs** + a **RangeCalendar** grid, with bidirectional
range coordination, validation, form integration, and ARIA/data attrs. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) and all live DOM concerns (focus, positioning, scroll, return-focus) are out of
scope. Dependencies #284 (DateRangeField), #287 (RangeCalendar), #289 (DatePicker) are all merged.

### Spec drift (resolved in this PR per the spec-synchronization rule)

The spec (`spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md`) predates the finalized dependency
APIs and will not compile as written. The user approved **amending the spec to match the real
APIs** (value-based fields) and **adding a preset API**. Confirmed-against-code corrections:

| Spec as written | Reality | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| `format: DateFormat`, `format_date`/`parse_date`, `start_input_text`/`parsed_start` | `DateFormat` does not exist; `date_field` is segmented and emits `Option<CalendarDate>` (fields: `value`, `min_value`, `max_value`, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`; no `format`) | Drop `format`/text model. Store `start_date`/`end_date: Option<CalendarDate>`. Events `StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` / `EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` (mirror `date_range_field`) |
| `calendar::Props { is_range: true, range_start, range_end }` | `calendar::Props` has no `is_range`; range lives in the dedicated **`range_calendar`** component (#287) | `range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` |
| `CalendarDate::to_iso_string()` | Method is `to_iso8601()`; `DateRange::to_iso8601()` yields `"start/end"` | Use `range.to_iso8601()` for combined hidden input; `range.start.to_iso8601()` for split |
| `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(a,b)))` | `normalized()` returns `Option<DateRange>` (type error) | `match DateRange::normalized(a,b) { Some(r)=>set(Some(r)), None=>set(None) }` (mirror `date_range_field::recompute_range`) |
| No `today` prop | `range_calendar::Props.today` is required | Add `today: CalendarDate` prop, forward to `range_calendar_props()` (mirror `date_picker`) |
| `positioning: PositioningOptions` | Type exists but DatePicker omits it; positioning is an adapter concern per the issue's element-handling note | Drop from agnostic Props |
| Machine omits `type Effect`, no `SyncProps`, no `Clear` event | `Machine::Effect` is non-defaulted; controlled `value` + `ClearTrigger` part need wiring | Add `type Effect = NoEffect`, `Event::SyncProps(Box<Props>)` + `on_props_changed`, `Event::Clear` |

## Design

DateRangePicker owns the canonical range and popover state; it does **not** run child machines.
Like DatePicker, it exposes `*_props()` builders the adapter feeds to child machines and accepts
bridged events. The two-field value logic mirrors the proven `date_range_field`; the popover
open/close mirrors `date_picker`.

**State** (unchanged): `Closed`, `Open`.

**Events** (amended): `Open`, `Close`, `Toggle`, `SelectRangeComplete { range: DateRange }`,
`StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`, `EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`,
`SelectPreset { index: usize }`, `Clear`, `FocusIn`, `FocusOut`, `KeyDown { key: KeyboardKey }`,
`SyncProps(Box<Props>)`.

**Context** (amended): `value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>`, `open: Bindable<bool>`,
`start_date`/`end_date: Option<CalendarDate>`, `active_field: ActiveField`,
`min`/`max: Option<CalendarDate>`, `today: CalendarDate`, `presets: Vec<Preset>`,
`visible_months: usize`, `is_rtl: bool`, `locale: Locale`, `intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>`
(for `range_description` formatting), `messages: Messages`, `disabled`/`readonly`/`required`,
`force_leading_zeros: bool`, `has_description`/`has_error_message: bool`,
`name`/`start_name`/`end_name: Option<String>`, `ids: ComponentIds`.

**Props** (amended): `id`, `value: Option<Option<DateRange>>`, `default_value`, `min`, `max`,
`today` (default `CalendarDate::new_gregorian(2025,1,1)` like DatePicker), `presets: Vec<Preset>`,
`visible_months: usize` (default `2`), `is_rtl`, `disabled`, `readonly`, `required`,
`force_leading_zeros`, `has_description`, `has_error_message`, `name`, `start_name`, `end_name`,
`close_on_select: bool` (default `true`). Derives `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Default, HasId`.

**New public `Preset` type** (deterministic, no closures — consumer supplies a pre-localized
label + a concrete range computed relative to the same injected `today`):
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct Preset {
    pub label: String,
    pub range: DateRange,
}
```

**Anatomy `Part`** (amended — adds data-carrying `PresetTrigger`, mirroring
`range_calendar::Part::Row { week_index }`):
`Root, Label, Control, StartInput, Separator, EndInput, Trigger, ClearTrigger,
PresetTrigger { index: usize }, Positioner, Content, Description, ErrorMessage, HiddenInput`.

**Transition logic:**

- `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`: update the field, set `active_field`,
  recompute range via `DateRange::normalized` (None when incomplete/incomparable). Blocked when `readonly`.
- `Clear`: clear `value`/`start_date`/`end_date`.
- `KeyDown` Escape (Open) → Close; ArrowDown (Closed) → Open.
- `FocusOut` (Open) → Close. `FocusIn` → `None` (kept for adapter-symmetry/spec parity).
- `SyncProps`: re-apply controlled `value` via `Bindable::sync_controlled`; refresh cached
  `min`/`max`/`today`/`presets`/flags (mirror `date_range_field::sync_props`). `open` is always uncontrolled.

**Api** (amended, no live-DOM ops): `root_attrs` (incl. `data-ars-invalid` when range out of
bounds — reuse `date_range_field`'s `date_out_of_bounds` check), `label_attrs` (`for={id}-start`),
`control_attrs` (`role=group`), `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`),
`start_field_props()`/`end_field_props() -> date_field::Props` (real fields: `value`, `min_value`,
`max_value` bounded by the sibling field, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`, `invalid`),
`trigger_attrs`, `clear_trigger_attrs` (disabled when no value/disabled),
`preset_trigger_attrs(index)` + `presets()` + `preset_label(index)`,
`positioner_attrs`, `content_attrs` (`role=dialog`, `aria-modal=false`, `aria-labelledby`),
`description_attrs`, `error_message_attrs` (`role=alert`),
`hidden_input_attrs` (`range.to_iso8601()`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs`,
`range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` (`..Default::default()` + value/min/max/today/
visible_months/is_rtl), `range_description() -> Option<String>` (via shared `format_date_label`),
imperative `open/close/toggle/clear/is_open/selected_range`, and `on_*` dispatch helpers.
`ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatches every `Part`.

**Messages** (per spec §4.1, unchanged): `trigger_label`, `start_label`, `end_label`,
`range_description: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str,&str,&Locale)->String + Send + Sync>`, `clear_label`.
Add `preset` labels come from `Preset.label`, not Messages.

## Files

Create:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/mod.rs` — machine, `State`, `Event`,
  `ActiveField`, `Context`, `Props`, `Preset`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. Directory-backed module.

- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/snapshots/*.snap` — committed insta fixtures.

- `disabled` guard returns `None` for all events except `SyncProps` (mirror `date_range_field`).
- `Open`/`Toggle` (Closed) → Open; `Close`/`Toggle` (Open) → Closed.
- `SelectRangeComplete { range }`: set `start_date`/`end_date`/`value`; close iff `props.close_on_select`.
- `SelectPreset { index }`: look up `ctx.presets[index]`, apply its range like `SelectRangeComplete`.
- `StartValueChange`/`EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)`: update the field, set `active_field`,

- `Clear`: clear `value`/`start_date`/`end_date`.
- `KeyDown` Escape (Open) → Close; ArrowDown (Closed) → Open.
- `FocusOut` (Open) → Close. `FocusIn` → `None` (kept for adapter-symmetry/spec parity).
- `SyncProps`: re-apply controlled `value` via `Bindable::sync_controlled`; refresh cached
  `min`/`max`/`today`/`presets`/flags (mirror `date_range_field::sync_props`). `open` is always uncontrolled.

bounds — reuse `date_range_field`'s `date_out_of_bounds` check), `label_attrs` (`for={id}-start`),
`control_attrs` (`role=group`), `separator_attrs` (`aria-hidden`),
`start_field_props()`/`end_field_props() -> date_field::Props` (real fields: `value`, `min_value`,
`max_value` bounded by the sibling field, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`, `invalid`),

`preset_trigger_attrs(index)` + `presets()` + `preset_label(index)`,
`positioner_attrs`, `content_attrs` (`role=dialog`, `aria-modal=false`, `aria-labelledby`),
`description_attrs`, `error_message_attrs` (`role=alert`),
`hidden_input_attrs` (`range.to_iso8601()`), `start_hidden_input_attrs`/`end_hidden_input_attrs`,
`range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` (`..Default::default()` + value/min/max/today/
visible_months/is_rtl), `range_description() -> Option<String>` (via shared `format_date_label`),
imperative `open/close/toggle/clear/is_open/selected_range`, and `on_*` dispatch helpers.
`ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatches every `Part`.

**Messages** (per spec §4.1, unchanged): `trigger_label`, `start_label`, `end_label`,
`range_description: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&str,&str,&Locale)->String + Send + Sync>`, `clear_label`.
Add `preset` labels come from `Preset.label`, not Messages.

## Files

Create:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/mod.rs` — machine, `State`, `Event`,
  `ActiveField`, `Context`, `Props`, `Preset`, `Part`, `Api`, `Messages`. Directory-backed module.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/tests.rs` — unit + snapshot tests.
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_picker/snapshots/*.snap` — committed insta fixtures.

Modify:
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/mod.rs` — add `pub mod date_range_picker;`; promote the
  duplicated private `format_date_label(date, &dyn IntlBackend, &Locale)` to a `pub(crate)` shared
  helper here and have `date_range_field`, `range_calendar`, and the new module call it (dedup;
  identical signature already exists in both siblings).
- `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_range_field/mod.rs` and `.../range_calendar/mod.rs` —
  use the shared `format_date_label` (remove their local copies).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/date_time.rs` — add
  `date_range_picker_anatomy_matches_spec` listing all 14 parts (use a default index for
  `PresetTrigger { index: 0 }`, mirroring how `Row`/`Cell` are listed).
- `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/date_time.rs` — add a `date_range_picker`
  arbitrary-event strategy + invariant block (mirror the existing `range_calendar`/`date_range_field` entries).
- `spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md` — rewrite §1.2 Events, §1.3 Context, §1.4 Props,
  §1.8 Machine, §1.9 Connect/API, §2 Anatomy (add `PresetTrigger`), and update §1 intro/§6 parity to
  match the value-based composition + the new `Preset` API; fix all the drift rows in the table above.

No `lib.rs`, `manifest.toml`, or catalog changes (component already specced/registered;
accessed via `ars_components::date_time::date_range_picker`).

## Workflow

1. Move issue #290 to **In Progress** on the GitHub Project board.
2. **TDD:** write the named tests first (unit + ARIA + snapshot) referencing the amended API, watch
   them fail, then implement `mod.rs` to green. Amend the spec alongside the implementation so §1/§2
   stay the contract.
3. Reuse, do not reinvent: `DateRange`/`CalendarDate`/`IntlBackend` from `ars_i18n`;
   `date_field::Props` (segmented fields); `range_calendar::Props`; `Bindable`,

   `date_range_field`'s `recompute_range`/`date_out_of_bounds`/`sync_props` patterns;
   the shared `format_date_label`.
4. Zero warnings, `missing_docs` satisfied on every public item.
5. **MANDATORY** `post-implementation-audit` skill (all three phases; every finding fixed in this PR).

8. Add `snapshot-reviewed` label (this PR creates `.snap` fixtures).
9. **MANDATORY** `waiting-for-codex-review` skill after first push and after each subsequent push.
10. Merge only after green CI + Codex 👍; then close #290.

Unit (transition): `initial_state_is_closed`; `open_and_close`; `toggle`;
`select_range_complete_sets_value_and_closes`; `select_range_complete_stays_open_when_not_close_on_select`;
`start_value_change_recomputes_range`; `end_value_change_recomputes_range`;
`field_edit_syncs_into_range` (value sync: editing a field updates the canonical range/calendar highlight);

`select_preset_applies_range_and_closes`; `clear_resets_value`; `escape_closes`; `arrow_down_opens`;
`focusout_closes`; `disabled_ignores_events`; `readonly_blocks_selection_allows_open`;

`content_dialog_attrs`; `error_message_alert_role`; `hidden_input_iso_range` (two hidden inputs:
combined `start/end` + split `start_name`/`end_name`); `start_field_props_bounds_and_label`;
`end_field_props_bounds_and_label`; `range_calendar_props_forwards_today_and_visible_months`;

# Task #290 — Implement DateRangePicker agnostic core

## Context

GitHub issue #290 (epic #224, 5 pts) asks for the framework-agnostic core of **DateRangePicker**:
a popover composing **two DateField inputs** + a **RangeCalendar** grid, with bidirectional
range coordination, validation, form integration, and ARIA/data attrs. Adapter components
(Leptos/Dioxus) and all live DOM concerns (focus, positioning, scroll, return-focus) are out of
scope. Dependencies #284 (DateRangeField), #287 (RangeCalendar), #289 (DatePicker) are all merged.

### Spec drift (resolved in this PR per the spec-synchronization rule)

The spec (`spec/components/date-time/date-range-picker.md`) predates the finalized dependency
APIs and will not compile as written. The user approved **amending the spec to match the real
APIs** (value-based fields) and **adding a preset API**. Confirmed-against-code corrections:

| Spec as written | Reality | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| `format: DateFormat`, `format_date`/`parse_date`, `start_input_text`/`parsed_start` | `DateFormat` does not exist; `date_field` is segmented and emits `Option<CalendarDate>` (fields: `value`, `min_value`, `max_value`, `aria_label`, `force_leading_zeros`; no `format`) | Drop `format`/text model. Store `start_date`/`end_date: Option<CalendarDate>`. Events `StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` / `EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>)` (mirror `date_range_field`) |
| `calendar::Props { is_range: true, range_start, range_end }` | `calendar::Props` has no `is_range`; range lives in the dedicated **`range_calendar`** component (#287) | `range_calendar_props() -> range_calendar::Props` |
| `CalendarDate::to_iso_string()` | Method is `to_iso8601()`; `DateRange::to_iso8601()` yields `"start/end"` | Use `range.to_iso8601()` for combined hidden input; `range.start.to_iso8601()` for split |
| `ctx.value.set(Some(DateRange::normalized(a,b)))` | `normalized()` returns `Option<DateRange>` (type error) | `match DateRange::normalized(a,b) { Some(r)=>set(Some(r)), None=>set(None) }` (mirror `date_range_field::recompute_range`) |
| No `today` prop | `range_calendar::Props.today` is required | Add `today: CalendarDate` prop, forward to `range_calendar_props()` (mirror `date_picker`) |

content.content:
1	//! DateRangeField component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! This module implements the framework-agnostic two-field date range input
4	//! defined in `spec/components/date-time/date-range-field.md`. It composes two
5	//! [`date_field`] instances (start and end) with shared range coordination,
6	//! min/max validation, focus-field tracking, and form integration. There is no
7	//! popover — the two fields render inline with a visual separator between them.
8	
9	#[cfg(test)]
10	mod tests;
11	
12	use alloc::{boxed::Box, format, string::String, sync::Arc, vec, vec::Vec};
13	use core::{
14	    cmp::Ordering,
15	    fmt::{self, Debug},
16	};
17	
18	use ars_core::{
19	    AriaAttr, AttrMap, Bindable, ComponentIds, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env,
20	    HtmlAttr, IntlBackend, Locale, MessageFn, NoEffect, TransitionPlan,
21	};
22	use ars_i18n::{CalendarDate, DateRange};
23	
24	use super::date_field;
25	
26	/// Identifies which of the two child fields is currently active.
27	///
28	/// Owned by `DateRangeField` as the first range component to require it; later
29	/// range components (e.g. `DateRangePicker`) reuse this enum rather than
30	/// redefining it.
31	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
32	pub enum ActiveField {
33	    /// The start field is active.
34	    Start,
35	
36	    /// The end field is active.
37	    End,
38	}
39	
40	/// States for the `DateRangeField` component.
41	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
42	pub enum State {
43	    /// Neither field holds focus.
44	    Idle,
45	
46	    /// The start field holds focus.
47	    StartFocused,
48	
49	    /// The end field holds focus.
50	    EndFocused,
51	}
52	
53	/// Events for the `DateRangeField` component.
54	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
55	pub enum Event {
56	    /// Focus moved to the start field.
57	    FocusStart,
58	
59	    /// Focus moved to the end field.
60	    FocusEnd,
61	
62	    /// Focus left the entire component.
63	    BlurAll,
64	
65	    /// Set the full range programmatically.
66	    SetRange(Option<DateRange>),
67	
68	    /// The start field's value changed.
69	    StartValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>),
70	
71	    /// The end field's value changed.
72	    EndValueChange(Option<CalendarDate>),
73	
74	    /// Reactive props changed; re-derive context from the new props.
75	    ///
76	    /// Emitted by [`Machine::on_props_changed`] so adapter-driven prop updates
77	    /// (controlled `value`, `min`/`max`, `disabled`, names, …) reach the
78	    /// context after mount.
79	    SyncProps(Box<Props>),
80	}
81	
82	/// Context for the `DateRangeField` component.
83	#[derive(Clone)]
84	pub struct Context {
85	    /// Controlled or uncontrolled range value.
86	    ///
87	    /// This is the *derived complete* range: it is `Some` only when both
88	    /// [`start_date`](Self::start_date) and [`end_date`](Self::end_date) are
89	    /// set, and is always normalized so `start <= end`.
90	    pub value: Bindable<Option<DateRange>>,
91	
92	    /// The start field's current value, tracked independently so a range can be
93	    /// assembled incrementally as each field changes.
94	    pub start_date: Option<CalendarDate>,
95	
96	    /// The end field's current value, tracked independently so a range can be
97	    /// assembled incrementally as each field changes.
98	    pub end_date: Option<CalendarDate>,
99	
100	    /// The active field, if either currently holds focus.
101	    pub active_field: Option<ActiveField>,
102	
103	    /// The minimum selectable date applied to both fields.
104	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
105	
106	    /// The maximum selectable date applied to both fields.
107	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
108	
109	    /// The resolved locale.
110	    pub locale: Locale,
111	
112	    /// Backend used for locale-dependent labels in range descriptions.
113	    pub intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend>,
114	
115	    /// Resolved translatable messages.
116	    pub messages: Messages,
117	
118	    /// Whether the component is disabled.
119	    pub disabled: bool,
120	
121	    /// Whether the component is read-only.
122	    pub readonly: bool,
123	
124	    /// Whether the component is required for form submission.
125	    pub required: bool,
126	
127	    /// Form field name for a single hidden input carrying the range value.
128	    pub name: Option<String>,
129	
130	    /// Form field name for a separate hidden input carrying the start date.
131	    pub start_name: Option<String>,
132	
133	    /// Form field name for a separate hidden input carrying the end date.
134	    pub end_name: Option<String>,
135	
136	    /// Derived component part ids.
137	    pub ids: ComponentIds,
138	
139	    /// Whether the `Description` part is rendered.
140	    pub has_description: bool,
141	
142	    /// Whether the `ErrorMessage` part is rendered.
143	    pub has_error_message: bool,
144	
145	    /// When `true`, numeric segments in both child fields display with leading
146	    /// zeros (e.g. "03" instead of "3").
147	    pub force_leading_zeros: bool,
148	}
149	
150	impl Debug for Context {
151	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
152	        f.debug_struct("Context")
153	            .field("value", &self.value)
154	            .field("start_date", &self.start_date)
155	            .field("end_date", &self.end_date)
156	            .field("active_field", &self.active_field)
157	            .field("min", &self.min)
158	            .field("max", &self.max)
159	            .field("locale", &self.locale)
160	            .field("intl_backend", &"<dyn IntlBackend>")
161	            .field("messages", &self.messages)
162	            .field("disabled", &self.disabled)
163	            .field("readonly", &self.readonly)
164	            .field("required", &self.required)
165	            .field("name", &self.name)
166	            .field("start_name", &self.start_name)
167	            .field("end_name", &self.end_name)
168	            .field("ids", &self.ids)
169	            .field("has_description", &self.has_description)
170	            .field("has_error_message", &self.has_error_message)
171	            .field("force_leading_zeros", &self.force_leading_zeros)
172	            .finish()
173	    }
174	}
175	
176	impl Context {
177	    /// Returns `true` when the current range violates the configured min/max
178	    /// bounds.
179	    ///
180	    /// The stored range always satisfies `start <= end` (it is normalized on
181	    /// every value change), so the only remaining source of invalidity is a
182	    /// start earlier than `min` or an end later than `max`.
183	    #[must_use]
184	    pub fn is_invalid(&self) -> bool {
185	        let Some(range) = self.value.get() else {
186	            return false;
187	        };
188	
189	        // A normalized range (`start <= end`) lies within `[min, max]` iff
190	        // neither endpoint is out of bounds.
191	        date_out_of_bounds(Some(&range.start), self.min.as_ref(), self.max.as_ref())
192	            || date_out_of_bounds(Some(&range.end), self.min.as_ref(), self.max.as_ref())
193	    }
194	}
195	
196	/// Returns `true` when `date` is present and falls outside the inclusive
197	/// `[min, max]` bounds (either bound absent means unbounded on that side).
198	fn date_out_of_bounds(
199	    date: Option<&CalendarDate>,
200	    min: Option<&CalendarDate>,
201	    max: Option<&CalendarDate>,
202	) -> bool {
203	    let Some(date) = date else {
204	        return false;
205	    };
206	
207	    let below_min =
208	        min.is_some_and(|min| matches!(date.compare_within_calendar(min), Some(Ordering::Less)));
209	    let above_max =
210	        max.is_some_and(|max| matches!(date.compare_within_calendar(max), Some(Ordering::Greater)));
211	
212	    below_min || above_max
213	}
214	
215	/// Props for the `DateRangeField` component.
216	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, ars_core::HasId)]
217	pub struct Props {
218	    /// The stable DOM id for the component.
219	    pub id: String,
220	
221	    /// The externally controlled value of the component.
222	    ///
223	    /// `None` means uncontrolled. `Some(None)` means the parent controls the
224	    /// component as empty. `Some(Some(range))` means a concrete range.
225	    pub value: Option<Option<DateRange>>,
226	
227	    /// The initial value used when the component is uncontrolled.
228	    pub default_value: Option<DateRange>,
229	
230	    /// The minimum selectable date.
231	    pub min: Option<CalendarDate>,
232	
233	    /// The maximum selectable date.
234	    pub max: Option<CalendarDate>,
235	
236	    /// Whether the component is disabled.
237	    pub disabled: bool,
238	
239	    /// Whether the component is read-only.
240	    pub readonly: bool,
241	
242	    /// Whether the component is required for form submission.
243	    pub required: bool,
244	
245	    /// Form field name for a single hidden input carrying the range value.
246	    pub name: Option<String>,
247	
248	    /// Form field name for the start date (alternative to `name`).
249	    ///
250	    /// When set, the start date submits as a separate form field.
251	    pub start_name: Option<String>,
252	
253	    /// Form field name for the end date (alternative to `name`).
254	    ///
255	    /// When set, the end date submits as a separate form field.
256	    pub end_name: Option<String>,
257	
258	    /// When `true`, numeric segments in both child fields display with leading
259	    /// zeros. Defaults to `false`, which uses locale-aware formatting.
260	    pub force_leading_zeros: bool,
261	
262	    /// Whether a `Description` element is rendered. When `true`, the root's
263	    /// `aria-describedby` references the description part id.
264	    pub has_description: bool,
265	
266	    /// Whether an `ErrorMessage` element is rendered. When `true`, the root's
267	    /// `aria-describedby` references the error-message part id.
268	    pub has_error_message: bool,
269	}
270	
271	impl Props {
272	    /// Returns a fresh [`Props`] value with the `DateRangeField` defaults.
273	    #[must_use]
274	    pub fn new() -> Self {
275	        Self::default()
276	    }
277	
278	    /// Sets [`id`](Self::id), the stable DOM id for the component.
279	    #[must_use]
280	    pub fn id(mut self, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
281	        self.id = id.into();
282	        self
283	    }
284	
285	    /// Sets [`value`](Self::value), the externally controlled range value.
286	    #[must_use]
287	    pub fn value(mut self, value: Option<DateRange>) -> Self {
288	        self.value = Some(value);
289	        self
290	    }
291	
292	    /// Sets [`default_value`](Self::default_value), the uncontrolled initial range.
293	    #[must_use]
294	    pub fn default_value(mut self, value: Option<DateRange>) -> Self {
295	        self.default_value = value;
296	        self
297	    }
298	
299	    /// Sets [`min`](Self::min), the minimum selectable date.
300	    #[must_use]
301	    pub fn min(mut self, value: Option<CalendarDate>) -> Self {
302	        self.min = value;
303	        self
304	    }
305	
306	    /// Sets [`max`](Self::max), the maximum selectable date.
307	    #[must_use]
308	    pub fn max(mut self, value: Option<CalendarDate>) -> Self {
309	        self.max = value;
310	        self
311	    }
312	
313	    /// Sets [`disabled`](Self::disabled), whether the component is disabled.
314	    #[must_use]
315	    pub const fn disabled(mut self, disabled: bool) -> Self {
316	        self.disabled = disabled;
317	        self
318	    }
319	
320	    /// Sets [`readonly`](Self::readonly), whether the component is read-only.
321	    #[must_use]
322	    pub const fn readonly(mut self, readonly: bool) -> Self {
323	        self.readonly = readonly;
324	        self
325	    }
326	
327	    /// Sets [`required`](Self::required), whether the component is required.
328	    #[must_use]
329	    pub const fn required(mut self, required: bool) -> Self {
330	        self.required = required;
331	        self
332	    }
333	
334	    /// Sets [`name`](Self::name), the combined hidden input form name.
335	    #[must_use]
336	    pub fn name(mut self, name: Option<String>) -> Self {
337	        self.name = name;
338	        self
339	    }
340	
341	    /// Sets [`start_name`](Self::start_name), the separate start-date form name.
342	    #[must_use]
343	    pub fn start_name(mut self, name: Option<String>) -> Self {
344	        self.start_name = name;
345	        self
346	    }
347	
348	    /// Sets [`end_name`](Self::end_name), the separate end-date form name.
349	    #[must_use]
350	    pub fn end_name(mut self, name: Option<String>) -> Self {
351	        self.end_name = name;
352	        self
353	    }
354	
355	    /// Sets [`force_leading_zeros`](Self::force_leading_zeros).
356	    #[must_use]
357	    pub const fn force_leading_zeros(mut self, force: bool) -> Self {
358	        self.force_leading_zeros = force;
359	        self
360	    }
361	
362	    /// Sets [`has_description`](Self::has_description).
363	    #[must_use]
364	    pub const fn has_description(mut self, has_description: bool) -> Self {
365	        self.has_description = has_description;
366	        self
367	    }
368	
369	    /// Sets [`has_error_message`](Self::has_error_message).
370	    #[must_use]
371	    pub const fn has_error_message(mut self, has_error_message: bool) -> Self {
372	        self.has_error_message = has_error_message;
373	        self
374	    }
375	}
376	
377	/// Closure type for the range description message, given the formatted start
378	/// and end date strings plus the active locale.
379	type RangeDescriptionFn = dyn Fn(&str, &str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
380	
381	/// Translatable messages for the `DateRangeField` component.
382	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
383	pub struct Messages {
384	    /// Accessible label for the start date field.
385	    pub start_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
386	
387	    /// Accessible label for the end date field.
388	    pub end_label: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
389	
390	    /// Text rendered between the start and end fields.
391	    pub separator_text: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
392	
393	    /// Screen-reader description of the full range, given formatted start and
394	    /// end date strings.
395	    pub range_description: MessageFn<RangeDescriptionFn>,
396	}
397	
398	impl Default for Messages {
399	    fn default() -> Self {
400	        Self {
401	            start_label: MessageFn::static_str("Start date"),
402	            end_label: MessageFn::static_str("End date"),
403	            separator_text: MessageFn::static_str(" \u{2013} "),
404	            range_description: MessageFn::new(|start: &str, end: &str, _locale: &Locale| {
405	                format!("{start} to {end}")
406	            }),
407	        }
408	    }
409	}
410	
411	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
412	
413	/// State machine for the `DateRangeField` component.
414	#[derive(Debug)]
415	pub struct Machine;
416	
417	impl ars_core::Machine for Machine {
418	    type State = State;
419	    type Event = Event;
420	    type Context = Context;
421	    type Props = Props;
422	    type Messages = Messages;
423	    type Effect = NoEffect;
424	    type Api<'a> = Api<'a>;
425	
426	    fn init(
427	        props: &Self::Props,
428	        env: &Env,
429	        messages: &Self::Messages,
430	    ) -> (Self::State, Self::Context) {
431	        let value = if let Some(value) = &props.value {
432	            Bindable::controlled(value.clone())
433	        } else {
434	            Bindable::uncontrolled(props.default_value.clone())
435	        };
436	
437	        let initial_range = value.get().clone();
438	        let start_date = initial_range.as_ref().map(|range| range.start.clone());
439	        let end_date = initial_range.as_ref().map(|range| range.end.clone());
440	
441	        let ctx = Context {
442	            value,
443	            start_date,
444	            end_date,
445	            active_field: None,
446	            min: props.min.clone(),
447	            max: props.max.clone(),
448	            locale: env.locale.clone(),
449	            intl_backend: Arc::clone(&env.intl_backend),
450	            messages: messages.clone(),
451	            disabled: props.disabled,
452	            readonly: props.readonly,
453	            required: props.required,
454	            name: props.name.clone(),
455	            start_name: props.start_name.clone(),
456	            end_name: props.end_name.clone(),
457	            ids: ComponentIds::from_id(&props.id),
458	            has_description: props.has_description,
459	            has_error_message: props.has_error_message,
460	            force_leading_zeros: props.force_leading_zeros,
461	        };
462	
463	        (State::Idle, ctx)
464	    }
465	
466	    fn transition(
467	        _state: &Self::State,
468	        event: &Self::Event,
469	        ctx: &Self::Context,
470	        _props: &Self::Props,
471	    ) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Self>> {
472	        // `SyncProps` must process even when disabled so adapter-driven prop
473	        // updates (including re-enabling) still reach the context.
474	        if ctx.disabled && !matches!(event, Event::SyncProps(_)) {
475	            return None;
476	        }
477	
478	        match event {
479	            Event::SyncProps(props) => {
480	                let props = props.as_ref().clone();
481	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
482	                    sync_props(ctx, &props);
483	                }))
484	            }
485	
486	            Event::FocusStart => Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::StartFocused).apply(
487	                |ctx: &mut Context| {
488	                    ctx.active_field = Some(ActiveField::Start);
489	                },
490	            )),
491	
492	            Event::FocusEnd => Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::EndFocused).apply(
493	                |ctx: &mut Context| {
494	                    ctx.active_field = Some(ActiveField::End);
495	                },
496	            )),
497	
498	            Event::BlurAll => Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Idle).apply(|ctx: &mut Context| {
499	                ctx.active_field = None;
500	            })),
501	
502	            Event::SetRange(range) => {
503	                if ctx.readonly {
504	                    return None;
505	                }
506	
507	                let range = range.clone();
508	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
509	                    ctx.start_date = range.as_ref().map(|range| range.start.clone());
510	                    ctx.end_date = range.as_ref().map(|range| range.end.clone());
511	                    ctx.value.set(range);
512	                }))
513	            }
514	
515	            Event::StartValueChange(date) => {
516	                if ctx.readonly {
517	                    return None;
518	                }
519	
520	                let date = date.clone();
521	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
522	                    apply_start_change(ctx, date);
523	                }))
524	            }
525	
526	            Event::EndValueChange(date) => {
527	                if ctx.readonly {
528	                    return None;
529	                }
530	
531	                let date = date.clone();
532	                Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
533	                    apply_end_change(ctx, date);
534	                }))
535	            }
536	        }
537	    }
538	
539	    fn on_props_changed(old: &Self::Props, new: &Self::Props) -> Vec<Self::Event> {
540	        debug_assert_eq!(
541	            old.id, new.id,
542	            "date_range_field::Props.id must remain stable after init"
543	        );
544	
545	        if old == new {
546	            Vec::new()
547	        } else {
548	            vec![Event::SyncProps(Box::new(new.clone()))]
549	        }
550	    }
551	
552	    fn connect<'a>(
553	        state: &'a Self::State,
554	        ctx: &'a Self::Context,
555	        props: &'a Self::Props,
556	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Self::Event),
557	    ) -> Self::Api<'a> {
558	        Api::new(state, ctx, props, send)
559	    }
560	}
561	
562	/// Re-derives mutable context fields from changed props.
563	///
564	/// `id` is fixed at construction (see [`Machine::on_props_changed`]). For a
565	/// controlled component (`props.value` is `Some`) the controlled override and
566	/// the per-field working values are reconciled to the new range; an
567	/// uncontrolled component keeps its internal working state and merely drops any
568	/// stale controlled override.
569	fn sync_props(ctx: &mut Context, props: &Props) {
570	    ctx.min = props.min.clone();
571	    ctx.max = props.max.clone();
572	    ctx.disabled = props.disabled;
573	    ctx.readonly = props.readonly;
574	    ctx.required = props.required;
575	    ctx.name = props.name.clone();
576	    ctx.start_name = props.start_name.clone();
577	    ctx.end_name = props.end_name.clone();
578	    ctx.has_description = props.has_description;
579	    ctx.has_error_message = props.has_error_message;
580	    ctx.force_leading_zeros = props.force_leading_zeros;
581	
582	    if let Some(controlled) = &props.value {
583	        ctx.value.sync_controlled(Some(controlled.clone()));
584	        ctx.start_date = controlled.as_ref().map(|range| range.start.clone());
585	        ctx.end_date = controlled.as_ref().map(|range| range.end.clone());
586	    } else {
587	        ctx.value.sync_controlled(None);
588	    }
589	}
590	
591	/// Applies a start-date change, then recomputes the derived range.
592	fn apply_start_change(ctx: &mut Context, start: Option<CalendarDate>) {
593	    ctx.start_date = start;
594	
595	    recompute_range(ctx);
596	}
597	
598	/// Applies an end-date change, then recomputes the derived range.
599	fn apply_end_change(ctx: &mut Context, end: Option<CalendarDate>) {
600	    ctx.end_date = end;
601	
602	    recompute_range(ctx);
603	}
604	
605	/// Recomputes the derived complete range from the two field values.
606	///
607	/// When both fields hold a value the range is normalized (swapping if needed so
608	/// `start <= end`) and the normalized order is reflected back into the field
609	/// values. When either field is empty the range is incomplete and the value is
610	/// cleared.
611	fn recompute_range(ctx: &mut Context) {
612	    match (ctx.start_date.clone(), ctx.end_date.clone()) {
613	        (Some(start), Some(end)) => match DateRange::normalized(start, end) {
614	            Some(range) => {
615	                ctx.start_date = Some(range.start.clone());
616	                ctx.end_date = Some(range.end.clone());
617	                ctx.value.set(Some(range));
618	            }
619	
620	            // The two dates are not comparable (e.g. different calendars/eras
621	            // supplied via the public setters); clear the derived range rather
622	            // than leave a stale value behind the new field values.
623	            None => ctx.value.set(None),
624	        },
625	
626	        _ => ctx.value.set(None),
627	    }
628	}

634	    /// The root container grouping both fields.
635	    Root,
636	
637	    /// The shared label for the entire range.
638	    Label,

641	    StartField,
642

644	    Separator,
645	
646	    /// The wrapper around the end `DateField`.
647	    EndField,
648	
649	    /// The optional description element.
650	    Description,
651	
652	    /// The optional error message element.
653	    ErrorMessage,
654	
655	    /// The combined hidden form input.
656	    HiddenInput,
657	}

703	    /// Returns attributes for the root element.
704	    #[must_use]
705	    pub fn root_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
706	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
707	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::Root.data_attrs();
708	
709	        attrs
710	            .set(scope_attr, scope_val)
711	            .set(part_attr, part_val)
712	            .set(HtmlAttr::Id, self.ctx.ids.id())
713	            .set(HtmlAttr::Role, "group")
714	            .set(
715	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::LabelledBy),
716	                self.ctx.ids.part("label"),
717	            )
718	            .set(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-state"), self.state_name());
719	
720	        let mut described_by = Vec::new();
721	
722	        if self.ctx.has_description {
723	            described_by.push(self.ctx.ids.part("description"));
724	        }
725	
726	        if self.ctx.has_error_message {
727	            described_by.push(self.ctx.ids.part("error-message"));
728	        }
729	
730	        if !described_by.is_empty() {
731	            attrs.set(
732	                HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::DescribedBy),
733	                described_by.join(" "),
734	            );
735	        }
736	
737	        if self.ctx.disabled {
738	            attrs
739	                .set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-disabled"), true)
740	                .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Disabled), "true");
741	        }
742	
743	        if self.ctx.readonly {
744	            attrs
745	                .set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-readonly"), true)
746	                .set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ReadOnly), "true");
747	        }
748	
749	        if self.ctx.required {
750	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::Required), "true");
751	        }
752	
753	        if self.ctx.is_invalid() {
754	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-invalid"), true);
755	        }
756	
757	        attrs
758	    }

843	    /// Builds the child [`date_field::Props`] for the start date input.
844	    ///
845	    /// Both child fields are bounded only by the global `min`/`max`, never by
846	    /// the opposite endpoint: a child `DateField` clamps completed dates to its
847	    /// own `max_value`/`min_value`, so binding the start field to the current
848	    /// end date would clamp away an out-of-order edit before the parent can
849	    /// perform the documented swap. Cross-field ordering is enforced by
850	    /// [`recompute_range`] when the changed value reaches the parent, not by the
851	    /// child's clamp. `invalid` reflects whether this field's own value lies
852	    /// outside the global bounds.
853	    #[must_use]
854	    pub fn start_field_props(&self) -> date_field::Props {
855	        date_field::Props {
856	            id: self.ctx.ids.part("start"),
857	            value: Some(self.ctx.start_date.clone()),
858	            min_value: self.ctx.min.clone(),
859	            max_value: self.ctx.max.clone(),
860	            disabled: self.ctx.disabled,
861	            readonly: self.ctx.readonly,
862	            required: self.ctx.required,
863	            invalid: date_out_of_bounds(
864	                self.ctx.start_date.as_ref(),
865	                self.ctx.min.as_ref(),
866	                self.ctx.max.as_ref(),
867	            ),
868	            aria_label: Some((self.ctx.messages.start_label)(&self.ctx.locale)),
869	            force_leading_zeros: self.ctx.force_leading_zeros,
870	            ..date_field::Props::default()
871	        }
872	    }
873

876	    /// Bounded only by the global `min`/`max` for the same reason as
877	    /// [`Api::start_field_props`]; `invalid` reflects whether the end field's
878	    /// own value lies outside the global bounds.

880	    pub fn end_field_props(&self) -> date_field::Props {

1081	        match self.state {
1082	            State::Idle => "idle",

1096	            Part::StartField => self.start_field_attrs(),
1097	            Part::Separator => self.separator_attrs(),
